accept: upsertTx accepts both []map[string]string and []interface{}-with-map-elements forms (or normalises before the assertion).
Test: build a snapshot, deserialize it, call upsertTx, assert dep edges land in the index.
If the canonical post-snapshot type is []interface{}, RenderState normalises before returning, AND upsertTx asserts that single canonical type.
payload.description: Found by code reviewer 2026-05-10 at internal/index/index.go:297. Code: 'if deps, ok := rendered["deps"].([]map[string]string); ok { ... }'. Type assertion succeeds when deps came from a live fold (applyAddDep stores []map[string]string). Fails silently when deps came from a snapshot deserialization, where JSON round-trip yields []interface{} with map[string]interface{} elements. If upsertTx is ever called with snapshot-derived state (e.g. after compaction reads a snapshot back), no dep edges get written to the index. act ready and similar dep-aware queries would silently lose data.
payload.title: deps type mismatch in upsertTx after snapshot deserialization
